title: The Gloria Scott
synopsis: Holmes recounts to Watson the very first case he was ever engaged upon, involving his college friend Victor Trevor and the mysterious terror that a cryptic note inspired in Trevor's father, an otherwise robust country justice of the peace. The story unravels a decades-old secret about a mutiny aboard a convict ship, the Gloria Scott.
solution: Trevor's father was originally a convict transported on the Gloria Scott who survived a mutiny and assumed a new identity in England; Hudson the blackmailer had been a fellow convict, and the cryptic note — read by taking every third word — was a threat that destroyed the old man; he dies of the shock and the secret dies with him.
notable_elements: Holmes's very first case, predating his meeting with Watson
Features a cipher concealed within an apparently innocent letter — every third word forms the real message
A tale of a transported convict who rebuilt his life under a false identity
The Gloria Scott mutiny is a story-within-a-story, told through Trevor's father's confession letter
Establishes Holmes's origins as a consulting detective
